title: AIDA marketing model
citation: AIDA model: Attention, Interest, Desire, Action; use as a marketing-stage framework, not as direct support for every visual cue.
notes: Use only as a customer-side stage skeleton.

title: OpenStax Principles of Marketing: The Consumer Purchasing Decision Process
citation: OpenStax, Principles of Marketing, Section 3.3, The Consumer Purchasing Decision Process.
notes: Use for customer buying-stage and evaluation behavior support; do not ingest the textbook into model training.

title: OpenStax Principles of Marketing: Factors That Influence Consumer Buying Behavior
citation: OpenStax, Principles of Marketing, Section 3.2, Factors That Influence Consumer Buying Behavior.
notes: Use for persona and companion-influence interpretation; do not ingest the textbook into model training.

title: OpenStax Principles of Marketing: Steps in the Personal Selling Process
citation: OpenStax, Principles of Marketing, Section 15.3, Steps in the Personal Selling Process.
notes: Use for sales-action timing and low-pressure intervention support; do not ingest the textbook into model training.

title: SPIN Selling
notes: Use as citation-level support for consultative questioning; do not crawl or store book text.

title: Business Communication for Success: Nonverbal Strategies for Success
citation: Business Communication for Success, Section 11.5, Nonverbal Strategies for Success with Your Audience.
notes: Use only for general business-communication support for nonverbal cue interpretation; not direct retail-sales outcome evidence.

citation: Brehm, J. W. (1966). A Theory of Psychological Reactance. New York, NY: Academic Press.
notes: Candidate source for pressure/backfire mechanisms; store compact paraphrases only, no book text.
title: A Theory of Psychological Reactance

citation: Babin, B. J., Darden, W. R., & Griffin, M. (1994). Work and/or Fun: Measuring Hedonic and Utilitarian Shopping Value. Journal of Consumer Research, 20(4), 644-656. https://doi.org/10.1086/209376
notes: Candidate source for intent-tier distillation; store compact paraphrases only, no article text.
title: Work and/or Fun: Measuring Hedonic and Utilitarian Shopping Value

citation: Bellenger, D. N., & Korgaonkar, P. K. (1980). Profiling the Recreational Shopper. Journal of Retailing, 56(3), 77-92.
notes: Candidate source for recreational vs task-oriented shopping distinctions; store compact paraphrases only.
title: Profiling the Recreational Shopper

citation: Babin, B. J., Gonzalez, C., & Watts, C. (2007). Does Santa Have a Great Job? Gift Shopping Value and Satisfaction. Psychology & Marketing, 24(10), 895-917.
notes: Used to support gift_buyer_uncertain as a gift-shopping-specific intent prior; store compact paraphrases only.
title: Does Santa Have a Great Job? Gift Shopping Value and Satisfaction

citation: Arnold, M. J., & Reynolds, K. E. (2003). Hedonic Shopping Motivations. Journal of Retailing, 79(2), 77-95. https://doi.org/10.1016/S0022-4359(03)00007-1
notes: Used as a more accessible support for hedonic/recreational browsing than the Bellenger secondary citation alone.
title: Hedonic Shopping Motivations

citation: Internal PIWM expert questionnaire intake, 2026-05, three anonymized Maotai retail sales respondents A/B/C.
notes: Structured internal questionnaire source. Store only anonymized structured records and compact paraphrases; manual review required before converting into rules.
title: Maotai gold sales expert questionnaire structured intake
